Weekly status

Owner: Kelly West
Linked: [[weekly-status-notes]]

## Outline
- Background
- Decisions
- Open questions

## Next
- Draft v2 next week

Q2 Counseling Goals

Owner: Kelly West

## Program Objectives
- Increase student check-in participation by 15%
- Complete individual academic plans for all 8th graders by end of quarter
- Host two parent information sessions on high school transitions

## Social-Emotional Learning
- Roll out new SEL small-group curriculum for 6th graders
- Coordinate with teachers on classroom guidance lessons

## College & Career Readiness
- Schedule college & career night for 8th-grade families
- Invite local professionals for career panel

## Notes
- Check in with Dr. Fredericks on caseload numbers mid-quarter

College Night Planning — 8th Grade College & Career Night

Owner: Kelly West
Date: Spring semester

## Purpose
- Introduce 8th-grade students and families to high school pathways and post-secondary options

## Key Tasks
- [ ] Reserve school cafeteria or gym for evening event
- [ ] Reach out to Henrico County high school counselors for panelists
- [ ] Invite 2-3 local professionals for career Q&A
- [ ] Create family-friendly handout on course selection
- [ ] Translate materials into Spanish for ESL families

## Logistics
- Estimated attendance: 80-100 families
- Need volunteer sign-ups from PTA
- Light refreshments (check with front office on budget)

## Open Questions
- Confirm date with Dr. Fredericks
- Coordinate with 8th-grade team teachers

End of Semester Review

Owner: Kelly West
Semester: Fall 2025

## What Went Well
- Student check-in attendance was strong in October and November
- New small-group SEL sessions for anxious 6th graders had positive feedback
- Parent communication improved with the new monthly newsletter format

## What to Change
- Need a better system for tracking student referrals — spreadsheet is getting unwieldy
- Holiday stress period in December needs more proactive outreach to students
- Coordinate earlier with teachers before report card season

## Highlights
- Three students connected to outside counseling resources this semester
- Collaborated with Renee's contact at Richmond City Schools on shared resources

## Next Semester Priorities
- Launch 8th-grade college & career night planning in January
- Refresh classroom guidance lesson plan for 7th grade

Reading List

## Currently Reading
- *When Breath Becomes Air* — Paul Kalanithi (re-read, so good)
- *Upstream* — Dan Heath

## Recently Finished
- *Wild* — Cheryl Strayed
- *Being Mortal* — Atul Gawande
- *Devotions* — Mary Oliver (poetry, keep by the bed)

## Want to Read
- *Educated* — Tara Westover
- *Braiding Sweetgrass* — Robin Wall Kimmerer
- *The Faraway Nearby* — Rebecca Solnit
- *Tiny Beautiful Things* — Cheryl Strayed

## Notes
- Mary Oliver's "The Summer Day" is the one I keep coming back to
- Atul Gawande writes about caregiving in a way that hits close to home

Outdoor Time

## Regular Spots
- Byrd Park loop — favorite fall walk, especially when the leaves are turning
- Neighborhood streets around Westhampton — good for early evening runs with Greg
- Maymont when we want something more scenic on weekends

## Recent Outings
- Walked the Byrd Park lake trail three times this week, ~45 min each
- Saturday morning run with Greg, did about 3 miles, legs felt good
- Took the kids to Byrd Park on Sunday, Owen fed the ducks

## Ideas
- Try the Capital Trail section near Dock Street with Greg
- Plan a fall hike at Pocahontas State Park before it gets too cold

## Notes
- Getting outside after school makes the evenings so much better
- The light at Byrd Park in late afternoon in October is just beautiful

Music Journal

## Lately Listening
- Bon Iver — *For Emma, Forever Ago* (always, every fall)
- Phoebe Bridgers — *Punisher* (cannot stop)
- Iron & Wine — *Our Endless Numbered Days*
- Watchhouse (Mandolin Orange) — *Watchhouse*

## Guitar
- Still working on "Holocene" (Bon Iver) — the fingerpicking pattern is getting there
- Learned a basic version of "Savior Complex" (Phoebe Bridgers) this month
- Greg said he wants to learn to play too, thinking about getting him a beginner acoustic

## Playlists
- Made a fall walks playlist: Bon Iver, Sufjan Stevens, The National, Phoebe Bridgers
- Play it on Byrd Park walks, makes everything feel cinematic

## Notes
- Music is the best way to decompress after a hard day at school
- Nora asked me to teach her a chord — good project for winter break

Trip planning

- Captured on 2025-12-06.
- Status: idea, revisit on Sunday.
